_readme: 1. Copy this file and rename the copy to 'tetra_inbox.json' (same folder).
2. Delete this _TEMPLATE block and the example ops below.
3. Add your own ops (as many as you want in one file).
4. Save as tetra_inbox.json — SlowLoop picks it up on the next nightly run,
applies all ops, then moves the file to tetra_archive/ automatically.
ONE FILE = AS MANY OPS AS YOU WANT.
Each op is one object in the array. Mix and match ADD / UPDATE / RETIRE / MUTATE.

_ops_available.UPDATE: Change llm_score / llm_priority / llm_notes on an existing hypothesis.
_ops_available.RETIRE: Mark an existing hypothesis as retired (stops it being promoted).
_ops_available.MUTATE: Create a new hypothesis derived from a parent — generation counter increments.

_llm_priority_values.explore: New idea, low confidence — worth a quick test run.
_llm_priority_values.test: Promising — allocate evidence budget to this one.
_llm_priority_values.retire: Flag for deprecation after this cycle.

_comment: EXAMPLE: Add a brand new hypothesis
name: your_hypothesis_name_here
description: One or two sentences describing what this hypothesis means strategically.
llm_notes: Why you think this is worth testing — reference failure clusters, trends, etc.

_comment: EXAMPLE: Add a second hypothesis — you can have as many ADDs as you want
name: second_hypothesis_name
description: Another hypothesis. Put as many of these blocks in the array as you need.
llm_notes: Rationale for why this is promising.

_comment: EXAMPLE: Update an existing hypothesis's score/priority/notes (no win_rate change)
name: exact_name_of_existing_hypothesis
llm_notes: Explanation of why this needs re-prioritising.

_comment: EXAMPLE: Retire an existing hypothesis outright
name: exact_name_of_hypothesis_to_retire

_comment: EXAMPLE: Mutate an existing hypothesis into a new variant
name: new_mutated_hypothesis_name
description: What changed vs the parent hypothesis.
llm_notes: Why this mutation is an improvement over the parent.
